action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 25's eval failed mid-inference (container exit 1) after ~13.5 minutes. Rank 0 was significantly slower than other ranks, suggesting either an OOM crash on a large image or severe load imbalance. Best official mIoU remains 0.5132 from iteration 16. Remaining budget: ~52 minutes and 4 iterations. Analysis: Training a new model is impossible in 52 minutes. The only lever is to run the proven 7-scale TTA inference again, but make it robust and better balanced so it completes successfully within the time budget. I will rewrite predict.py with (1) load balancing by sorting images by area and distributing round-robin across GPUs, (2) OOM fallback that skips offending scales for individual images, (3) keep max_size=3200 and the 7 proven scales. This should prevent the transient failure and finish in ~40–50 minutes. Decision: Write robust predict.py, run a 30-second smoke test on 5 images, then immediately submit eval.
action.command:
python -c "
import torch, sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')
from model import SegmentationModel
m = SegmentationModel(num_classes=150)
ckpt = torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu')
sd = ckpt['model'] if isinstance(ckpt, dict) and 'model' in ckpt else ckpt
m.load_state_dict({k[7:] if k.startswith('module.') else k: v for k, v in sd.items()}, strict=False)
m.cuda().eval()
with torch.inference_mode():
    print('Forward', m(torch.zeros(1,3,512,512).cuda()).shape)
print('OK')
" && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /workspace/smoke_test.json 2>&1 | head -n 20
